product_overview: CloudOptima is a B2B FinOps dashboard that helps users connect AWS read-only credentials, review cloud cost summaries, and identify waste through optimization scans and historical analytics. It provides actionable visibility into estimated spend, potential savings, and resource-level remediation opportunities.
core_goals: Authenticate with an email and password to access the platform
Configure AWS read-only credentials for cost analysis
Review summarized cloud spend, savings, and service breakdowns
Run optimization scans to identify wasted or idle resources
View historical cost trends and analytics
Save and verify AWS configuration for ongoing analysis

features.name: User Authentication
features.description: Users sign in or create an account with email and password to access their CloudOptima workspace.
user_flows: Navigate to / -> Toggle between login and registration mode -> Fill in email address -> Fill in password -> Submit the form -> See the authenticated dashboard access path
Navigate to / -> Toggle between login and registration mode -> Enter an invalid email format -> Fill in password -> Submit the form -> See validation feedback

features.name: Dashboard Summary
features.description: Users view a high-level overview of cloud costs, potential savings, resource counts, and service-level cost distribution.
user_flows: Navigate to /dashboard -> View estimated monthly bill -> View potential monthly savings -> View active resources count -> Review the cost breakdown by service chart

features.name: Cloud Cost Scanner
features.description: Users run a scan to find waste resources and inspect optimization recommendations with potential savings.
user_flows: Navigate to /dashboard/scanner -> Run a cost optimization scan -> Review recommendation cards with instance details and savings -> Select an optimization action -> See the optimization result reflected in the recommendations
Navigate to /dashboard/scanner -> Run a cost optimization scan -> Review flagged resources with resource name or ID, service status, idle reason, and potential savings

features.name: AWS Configuration Settings
features.description: Users enter AWS credentials, save them, and verify the connection before using the analytics features.
user_flows: Navigate to /dashboard/settings -> Fill in AWS Access Key ID -> Fill in AWS Secret Access Key -> Select an AWS region -> Save the AWS configuration -> Verify the connection

Navigate to /dashboard/settings -> Fill in AWS Access Key ID -> Fill in AWS Secret Access Key -> Select an AWS region -> Save the AWS configuration -> See that the configuration is stored for the session

features.name: Cost Trend Reports
features.description: Users inspect historical cost analytics through trend charts over time.
user_flows: Navigate to /dashboard/reports -> View historical bar and line charts representing monthly cost trends -> Compare cost changes across periods

routes.description: Historical cost trend reports and analytics

features.name: User Authentication
features.description: Login or sign up with email and password via Firebase
user_interactions: Click Sign In or Register button
Toggle between Login and Registration mode

user_interactions: See recommendation cards showing instance details and savings
Click "Optimize Now" button to apply optimization action

Fill AWS Secret Access Key input
